info.title: MagicTrust Internal API
info.description: The implemented MagicTrust Internal API v1. Authenticate with the `x-api-key` header using a database-backed API client key in the form `mt_live_<random-secret>`. The standard `Authorization` header is not used by these routes. Each operation lists its required API client scope. Mutating operations also require an `Idempotency-Key`; records remain replayable for 24 hours. Repeating the same key and request returns the original response with `Idempotency-Replayed: true`, while reusing a key for a different request returns `409 IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSED`. A processing-result completion delivery failure is retryable with the same key and is not stored until completion succeeds.

tags.name: Requests
tags.description: Create, retrieve, search, and update requests.

tags.name: Request Data
tags.description: Merge internal processing metadata into mutable request data.

tags.name: Comments
tags.description: Add public or internal request comments.

tags.name: Attachments
tags.description: Register, upload, and download private request attachments.

post.description: Creates a requester, privacy request, and REQUEST_CREATED event transactionally. Internal API requests start in SUBMITTED status.

submittedData.message: I want to access my data.

get.description: Returns stable cursor-paginated request summaries ordered by `createdAt DESC, id DESC`. Filters combine with AND. Email and phone searches use normalized deterministic hashes without decrypting requester records.

Fetches a request by internal id or publicId and returns its internal detail, including mutable data, events, comments, attachment metadata, and communication metadata. Encrypted requester fields and hashes are not returned.

Fetches a request by internal id or publicId and decrypts only the requester identity and immutable original submission needed by an authorized processor. The response is private and not cacheable. This read-only operation does not change request state or create audit events.

Cache-Control.description: Prevents caching of decrypted processing data.

post.description: Lower-level status mutation capability for advanced integrations. Sets any currently supported request status, updates completedAt for terminal statuses, and records STATUS_CHANGED. It validates the status enum but does not enforce workflow transition rules. Processors should normally report final outcomes through POST /api/v1/requests/{id}/processing-result instead of setting SUCCESS directly.

Reports the downstream business outcome without exposing arbitrary workflow transitions. For SUCCESS, MagicTrust owns workflow completion: it delivers the workflow-specific consumer notification, reuses existing completion artifacts on retry, and only then changes the request to SUCCESS. DATA_ACCESS completion permits zero public files and includes existing public files through secure access when present. If delivery fails, the request remains non-SUCCESS and the same Idempotency-Key and payload can safely retry. REJECTED uses the existing terminal status and audit behavior and does not send a success notification.

200.description: Outcome applied. For SUCCESS, completion delivery succeeded before the returned request became SUCCESS.

502.description: Required consumer completion delivery failed. The request remains non-SUCCESS; retry the identical request with the same Idempotency-Key.

error.message: Completion notification could not be delivered.

patch.description: Shallow-merges the supplied object into mutableData while preserving omitted keys. submittedData is never modified. Dangerous keys (`__proto__`, `prototype`, and `constructor`) are rejected at any depth.

post.description: Records a CUSTOM event without changing request status or mutable data. Names must begin with a letter, contain only uppercase letters, numbers, and underscores, be 3-80 characters, and not match a built-in event name. Data is limited to 16 KB and rejects dangerous keys at any depth.
Creates a PUBLIC or INTERNAL comment and the corresponding audit event.
Creates attachment metadata and its visibility-specific audit event. This endpoint does not upload binary content.

Uploads one file to the configured private storage provider and creates attachment metadata and an audit event. Maximum size is 10 MB. Allowed MIME types are application/json, text/csv, application/pdf, text/plain, and application/zip. Idempotency hashes safe file metadata and its checksum.

201.description: File uploaded and attachment metadata created.

get.description: Streams an attachment after verifying it belongs to the request and records ATTACHMENT_DOWNLOADED. No storage URL is exposed. The optional actorId query parameter remains accepted for compatibility, but API_CLIENT audit identity is derived from the authenticated client.

200.description: Attachment content. Content-Type and filename are taken from stored metadata.

post.description: Creates an outbound communication record, sends a plain-text email, updates it to SENT or FAILED, and creates EMAIL_SENT or EMAIL_FAILED. Responses expose only a masked recipient.

Sends a request update to the encrypted requester email without changing request status. Every email includes the publicId, current status, supplied public message, and tracking link. FILE_AVAILABLE also creates a hashed 30-minute consumer access token and includes its secure access link; no file is attached.

value.message: Your request is currently being processed.
A response file is available for your request.

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: Database-backed API client key: `mt_live_<random-secret>`. The deprecated INTERNAL_API_KEY fallback works only outside production.

IdempotencyReplayed.description: Present with value `true` when the stored response is replayed.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Unique operation key retained for 24 hours. Reuse only when retrying the same method, route, and payload.

TypeFilter.name: type
TypeFilter.description: One or more comma-separated request types.

StatusFilter.name: status
StatusFilter.description: One or more comma-separated request statuses.

EmailFilter.name: email
EmailFilter.description: Exact normalized email match using the requester email HMAC; records are not decrypted.

PhoneFilter.name: phone
PhoneFilter.description: Exact normalized phone match using the requester phone HMAC; records are not decrypted.

CreatedTo.name: createdTo
CreatedTo.description: Exclusive ISO-8601 datetime; must be after createdFrom.

UpdatedTo.name: updatedTo
UpdatedTo.description: Exclusive ISO-8601 datetime; must be after updatedFrom.

Cursor.name: cursor
Cursor.description: Opaque nextCursor from the previous response.

id.description: For API_CLIENT actions, MagicTrust derives actorId from the authenticated client and does not trust this value.

type.description: Must not be a built-in MagicTrust event name.

actorId.description: For API_CLIENT actions, MagicTrust derives actorId from the authenticated client.

BadRequest.description: Validation failed, Idempotency-Key is missing, or the storage provider is unsupported.

Unauthorized.description: The x-api-key header is missing or invalid.

Forbidden.description: The API client lacks the required scope.

error.message: API client is not authorized for this operation.

NotFound.description: The request or related attachment was not found.

IdempotencyConflict.description: The Idempotency-Key was already used for a different request.

error.message: Idempotency-Key was already used for a different request.

InternalError.description: The request could not be processed.

CommunicationFailed.description: The communication record was created but the email provider failed. The response is communication metadata, not a generic error object.

communication.subject: Your MagicTrust request was updated
communication.errorMessage: Email provider failed to send the message.
